title: Measuring lysosomal exocytosis by Flow cytometry
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cc17q","text":"Protocol for measuring lysosomal exocytosis by flow cytometry. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"eitm","text":"Staining buffer:  5% FCS in PBS (filtered)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1jv15","text":"Blocking buffer: 5% FCS in PBS containing FcR block at 1:100 dilution (Anti-Mouse CD16/CD32; BD Biosciences Cat# 553141, RRID:AB_394656)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8fger","text":"Antibody staining solution: Dilute 0.5 ul anti-mouse-LAMP1-AF647 (Rat-anti-mouse-LAMP-1-AF647\n(BioLegend Cat# 121610, RRID:AB_571990) ) in 25 ul staining buffer per sample. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2knqc","text":"Viability stain: eg Fixable Viability Dye eFluor-450, # 65-0863-18, eBioscience","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"etmr2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Seed cells as required and stimulate to induce exocytosis. Prepare ~ 5x10^5 cells per sample.
Detach cells. Keep all samples on ice from now on.
Resuspend in 75 ul blocking buffer (5% FCS in PBS - optional: containing FcR blocker) and incubate for 10 min on ice.
Add 25 ul anti-LAMP1-AF647 antibody staining solution containing 0.5 ul antibody and incubate for 30 min on ice. Protect from light.
Add 300 ul PBS and pellet cells.
Resuspend cells in 100 ul dead/live staining solution and incubate for 10 min on ice. Protect from light.
Add 300 ul PBS and pellet cells.
After removing doublets, analyse the mean and medium LAMP1 fluorescence intensity in the live cell gate.

Dead/live single-colour control To prepare a live/dead single stain control, heat half of the sample at 65 °C for 00:10:00 min. Put back on ice immediately, mix with non-heat treated cells and stain as above. LAMP1 single-colour control Remove part of the stimulated samples and omit dead/live stain.
creator: Susanne Herbst
